reasons: Only normalize_workdir has an active repository caller, and it is a thin alias for validate_cwd.
The allowlist, decision model, subprocess executor, renderer, and metadata builder have no active callers or direct tests.
The two-state allow/deny model cannot represent the S5 allow/prompt/forbidden boundary.
The argv-only classifier does not parse the shell-string and compound-command surface executed by the active Claude and Codex runtimes.
Activating the legacy allowlist would reject common current runtime commands and silently change source-run behavior.
Process execution and output handling are already owned by process_exec.py and shell_runtimes.py.
preserved_behavior: Working directories remain constrained to the resolved workspace by validate_cwd.
Protected file-write boundaries remain owned by path_policy.py.
Command execution remains owned by process_exec.py and shell_runtimes.py.
Existing execution metadata and runtime-trace artifacts are not reinterpreted as proof of an enforced command allowlist.
RC-038 must not broaden command execution or make a production-sandbox claim.

required_properties: separate policy evaluation from process execution
represent allow, prompt, and forbidden as distinct outcomes
preserve matched rule, matched prefix, justification, and fallback provenance
define deterministic behavior for no-match and unknown parsing
evaluate every effective segment of compound shell commands
distinguish requested permission or sandbox fields from effective policy decisions
emit policy facts into tool metadata and runtime traces
cover both command and file-action policy visibility without claiming production containment
out_of_scope: automatic reuse of legacy executable allowlists

rc038_acceptance: replace normalize_workdir with a direct validate_cwd import
prove zero remaining imports, exports, tests, and documentation claims for the legacy implementation
freeze workspace cwd rejection and current command execution behavior
correct active documentation so metadata visibility is not described as command-policy enforcement
pass safety-focused, mainline, local acceptance, full tests, and git diff --check
implementation_evidence: tests/test_command_safety_retirement.py freezes retirement and behavior boundaries
